meta.notes: Add new entries to any category. The bot will use these as its primary knowledge source. Keep answers concise — 2-4 paragraphs max.

firm.name: Burlington Consult
firm.focus: EB-1A Extraordinary Ability and EB-2 National Interest Waiver petitions
firm.differentiator: Both founders hold approved EB-1A petitions. The firm is led by practitioners who have been through the process themselves.
firm.disclaimer: Burlington Consult provides immigration strategy and advisory services. This does not constitute the practice of law and does not establish an attorney-client relationship.

dual_petition.name: EB-1A & EB-2 NIW Dual Petition
dual_petition.comparable_price: $35,000–$55,000 at leading U.S. immigration law firms
includes: Full EB-1A petition (I-140) — criterion-by-criterion argument construction
Full EB-2 NIW petition (I-140) — Dhanasar three-prong framework
Up to 12 recommendation letters drafted from scratch
Evidence architecture — full documentary record curated to USCIS standards
12-month RFE and NOID retainer at no additional charge
dual_petition.credit_ladder: $85 Profile Assessment (normally $113) → credited toward $169 consultation (normally $225) → credited toward engagement. Every fee rolls forward.

profile_assessment.name: Profile Assessment
profile_assessment.description: Written assessment of your professional record against EB-1A criteria and the NIW Dhanasar framework. Delivered within 48 hours. Fee credited toward consultation.

consultation.name: Consultation
consultation.description: 30-minute focused session covering your profile, the strongest available pathway, the most viable field of endeavour, and a direct assessment of eligibility. Fee credited toward engagement.

eb1a.name: EB-1A (Einstein Visa)
eb1a.description: The most distinguished employment-based visa the United States issues. Places holders in the same statutory classification as Nobel Prize laureates. No job offer, no employer sponsor, no labor market test.
eb1a.requirement: Must demonstrate sustained national or international acclaim by meeting at least 3 of 10 regulatory criteria, followed by a final merits determination.
criteria: Awards — nationally or internationally recognised prizes for excellence
Membership — in associations requiring outstanding achievements
Published material — about the petitioner in major trade or professional publications
Judging — participation as a judge of others' work in the field
Original contributions — of major significance in the field
Scholarly articles — in professional or major trade publications
Critical role — leading or critical role for distinguished organisations
High salary — relative to peers in the field in relevant country
Commercial success — in the performing arts
Display of work — at artistic exhibitions or showcases
eb1a.stat: Fewer than 5% of all employment-based green cards carry the EB-1A classification annually.

niw.name: EB-2 National Interest Waiver
niw.description: Self-petitioned pathway for professionals whose proposed work serves a demonstrable U.S. national interest. No employer sponsor or PERM labor certification required.
niw.requirement: Must satisfy the Dhanasar three-prong test.
dhanasar_prongs: Substantial merit and national importance of the proposed endeavour
The petitioner is well positioned to advance the endeavour
On balance, it is beneficial to the United States to waive the job offer requirement

pathways.dual_filing: Burlington files both EB-1A and NIW simultaneously as standard practice. This preserves the same priority date across both pathways, creates strategic redundancy, and allows a single evidence record to support both petitions.

timeline.letters: First drafts within 1 week of receiving materials
timeline.petition_draft: First complete petition draft within 2-3 weeks
timeline.filing: Review, legal review, and USCIS submission within 3-4 weeks
timeline.premium_processing: 15 business days for I-140 decision (Form I-907, $2,965 government fee)
timeline.rfe_retainer: 12 months from submission date, at no additional charge

political_moment.travel_ban: Presidential Proclamation 10998 (December 2025) imposed a partial entry suspension on Nigerian nationals but explicitly preserved case-by-case waivers for individuals whose entry serves U.S. national interests. The I-140 petition and entry restriction are separate legal instruments. An I-140 can be filed regardless. A strong EB-1A or NIW approval is itself the most direct evidence for the national interest exception.
political_moment.uscis_hold: USCIS Policy Memorandum PM-602-0194 placed a hold on final adjudication for nationals of travel restriction countries. Processing continues up to the point of final adjudication. RFEs are being actively issued. Legal challenges are proceeding in federal court.
political_moment.proposed_rules: DHS proposed rule RIN 1615-AC85 would raise evidentiary thresholds for EB-1A and NIW. Draft regulations anticipated in 2026. Not yet operative. Petitions filed now are assessed against current standards.
political_moment.i140_permanence: An approved I-140 is permanent, does not expire, and simply preserves optionality. The adjustment of status application can be filed later. The priority date is established the day USCIS receives the I-140.
political_moment.urgency: Every month of delay in filing is a month later in the queue. Filing now, under current standards, establishes the priority date and positions the petitioner for when the adjudicatory environment resolves.

faq.question: Do I need a job offer in the United States?
faq.answer: No. Both EB-1A and EB-2 NIW are self-petitioned pathways. The green card is tied to your expertise and record, not to any employer, institution, or role.

faq.question: I am a Nigerian national. Can I still file?
faq.answer: Yes. The I-140 petition and entry restriction are separate instruments. You can file regardless of the travel ban. An approved I-140 is permanent, does not expire, and is the strongest evidence for the national interest exception when the restriction is lifted.

faq.question: What does Burlington provide that I cannot do myself?
faq.answer: The EB-1A and NIW are self-petition pathways by design. Burlington's lead adviser filed and obtained approval on a self-petition, on first submission. What Burlington provides is the case architecture that determines whether a petition succeeds or produces a Request for Evidence. Evidence that a petitioner holds is frequently not in the format or framing USCIS expects.

faq.question: Are USCIS filing fees included?
faq.answer: No. USCIS government filing fees are payable directly to USCIS. Current standard I-140 fees: $715 plus $600 Asylum Programme Fee, totalling $1,315. Optional premium processing (Form I-907): $2,965.

faq.question: What is the field of endeavour?
faq.answer: The most consequential decision in any petition. It is the specific articulation of what the petitioner proposes to pursue in the United States and why that work constitutes a national interest. Burlington identifies and stress-tests this before any drafting begins. Errors at this stage are not correctable by adding stronger evidence later.

faq.question: Can Burlington accommodate flexible payment?
faq.answer: Yes. Burlington accommodates deferred payment arrangements, phased structures, and arrangements tied to specific milestones. No alternative arrangement affects the quality or scope of the work.

faq.question: What is the referral programme?
faq.answer: When you introduce a qualified candidate who engages Burlington at any fee level, that person receives whichever is greater: a flat $5,000 reduction or a 40% discount on the agreed fee. At the standard $20,000 rate, the referred client pays $12,000.
